exampleSentences: Anh chào đại ca hôm nay.
Em gọi đại ca bằng điện thoại.
Chị gặp đại ca ở nhà.
sentenceBreakdowns: Anh (he) + chào (greets) + đại ca (boss) + hôm nay (today)
Em (I) + gọi (call) + đại ca (boss) + bằng (by/with) + điện thoại (phone)
Chị (she) + gặp (meets) + đại ca (boss) + ở (at) + nhà (home)
notes:
Đại ca = big boss / big brother. It’s used for a man who has power, leads a group, or acts like the top guy (sometimes in a gangster vibe, sometimes jokingly).

It’s a noun: một đại ca, vị đại ca này. Often paired with đàn em (junior members) or used as a title when talking to someone you respect or want to flatter.

It can sound tough or playful depending on tone. Close synonyms: anh cả (eldest brother, more family/neutral), ông trùm (big boss, stronger and more serious).
notes_md:
**Đại ca** = big boss / big brother. It’s used for a man who has power, leads a group, or acts like the top guy (sometimes in a gangster vibe, sometimes jokingly).

It’s a **noun**: *một đại ca*, *vị đại ca này*. Often paired with **đàn em** (junior members) or used as a title when talking to someone you respect or want to flatter.

It can sound tough or playful depending on tone. Close synonyms: **anh cả** (eldest brother, more family/neutral), **ông trùm** (big boss, stronger and more serious).
exampleSentencesTranslated: I call the boss on the phone.
She meets the boss at home.
